text.title: Spec-Driven Debugging Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Spec-Driven Debugging methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Bug fix MUST start with a written failing spec (executable test) naming the symptom AND expected behaviour; coding-without-failing-test for bugs on hot paths is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a failing test, the fix has no regression guard; the bug returns on the next refactor.

rule.statement: Repro MUST be minimised to <=20 lines OR a single failing assertion; bug-specs with multi-page repros are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Long repros hide the actual cause and resist re-running; minimisation surfaces the offending interaction.

rule.statement: If the bug is a regression, `git bisect` MUST identify the offending commit before the fix; guessing the cause is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Bisect finds the cause in O(log n) commits; guessing trades cheap minutes of bisect for hours of speculation.

rule.statement: Fix MUST land in the same PR as the failing spec; spec turning red → green is the verification artefact.
rule.rationale: Separating the spec from the fix lets either land alone; co-located change ensures the test guards the fix forever.

rule.statement: After the fix, the spec MUST remain in the codebase as a permanent regression test; deleting the spec after fix is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Removing the spec lets the bug return silently; keeping it is the only durable defence.

rule.statement: Bug spec MUST name the symptom precisely (input + observed output + expected output); vague titles like 'fix bug in X' are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Precise naming prevents 'fixed' tickets that close without fixing the actual bug.

rule.statement: Bug spec MUST link back to the originating incident report, issue, or user message; orphan bug-fixes without provenance are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without a link, future operators cannot trace why a test exists or when it can be retired.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
